task.title: Configuring an ethtool coalesce settings by using the network RHEL system role

li.p: You have prepared the control node and the managed nodes.
The account you use to connect to the managed nodes has sudo permissions for these nodes.

context.p: Interrupt coalescing collects network packets and generates a single interrupt for multiple packets. This reduces interrupt load and maximizes throughput. You can automate the configuration of these settings in the NetworkManager connection profile by using the network RHEL system role.

note.p: You cannot use the network RHEL system role to update only specific values in an existing connection profile. The role ensures that a connection profile exactly matches the settings in a playbook. If a connection profile with the same name already exists, the role applies the settings from the playbook and resets all other settings in the profile to their defaults. To prevent resetting values, always specify the whole configuration of the network connection profile in the playbook, including the settings that you do not want to change.

step.cmd: Create a playbook file, for example, ~/playbook.yml, with the following content:

info.codeblock: --- - name: Configure the network hosts: managed-node-01.example.com tasks: - name: Ethernet connection profile with dynamic IP address settings and coalesce settings ansible.builtin.include_role: name: redhat.rhel_system_roles.network vars: network_connections: - name: enp1s0 type: ethernet autoconnect: yes ip: dhcp4: yes auto6: yes ethtool: coalesce: rx_frames: 128 tx_frames: 128 state: up
info.p: The settings specified in the example playbook include the following:

dd.p: Sets the number of RX frames.
Sets the number of TX frames.

info.p: For details about all variables used in the playbook, see the /usr/share/ansible/roles/rhel-system-roles.network/README.md file on the control node.
Note that this command only validates the syntax and does not protect against a wrong but valid configuration.

li.p: Display the current offload features of the network device:
li.codeblock: # ansible managed-node-01.example.com -m command -a 'ethtool -c enp1s0' managed-node-01.example.com | CHANGED | rc=0 >> ... rx-frames: 128 ... tx-frames: 128 ...
